name: Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic (1931–1935)
description: A plain red field with the name of the republic inscribed in gold in the upper hoist, in the Romanised Tajik script ('Ç. Ş. S. Toçikiston') and in Russian ('Tadzhikskaya SSR').
history: Adopted by the fourth congress of the republican soviets in February 1931, the first flag of the union-republic-level Tajik SSR (raised from autonomous status). It bore the republic's name in gold lettering and was replaced by a decree of 4 July 1935 that added a hammer and sickle.

features.role: Latin/Russian inscription 'Ç. Ş. S. Toçikiston' and 'Tadzhikskaya SSR'
features.symbolism: the name of the Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic
